title: Usage messages: deduplicate '(default true)' et al
body:
Remove hardcoded '(default: true)' strings from bool flags,
and '(default this-or-that)' from string flags.

First because it's unmaintainable duplication that would cause
confusion should someone ever change the default and not notice
the message.

Second, because cobra[1] already prints '(default XXXX)' for
all options with non-false non-nil default. So in each of
these cases, current podman help behavior is:

    $ podman login --help
    ...
       --tls-verify  Require HTTPS ... (default: true) (default true)

This PR eliminates that duplication.

 [1] actually spf13/pflag/flag.go

The only nontrivial one of these is start.go, where the default
for sigProxy depends on the --attach flag. Solution: change
the command-line default to false, and implement the new
conditional default in logic. Bonus: removed unnecessary
check, because now if sigProxy is set without --attach,
we can guarantee that it was done by the user. But please
pay close scrutiny to this particular section in case
there's something I missed.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM other then the question about the -1

Thank you all for helpful comments.

New force push: rebased, included @mheon's "instead of terminal" suggestion, and, as a non-squashed separate commit, a risky (IMO) change to the Cobra default for `memory-swappiness`: it is now `0` (zero). This makes Cobra not output the confusing '(default -1)' message in the --help. I believe I've found and fixed the only instance in the code base that references this command-line option, but I really have no idea what I'm doing so please feel free to tell me to back out this change.

I think we ought to leave the -1, but figure out why it's set to -1, and note in the description text that -1 is a valid value (to use the system default, or unlimited, or whatever it happens to mean)

@mheon, I agree. My previous change wasn't even working - it broke `podman run` on rootless. I've undone it, and simply changed the help message text:
```
  --memory-swappiness int    Tune container memory swappiness (0 to 100, or -1 for system default) (default -1)
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

All right -- how's [this](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2633/commits/1e124306dbd35a4cfdf3f585119a2c4441ec543d)? I've cleared the Cobra default in all three offending cases, improved (I believe) the usage message, and (fingers crossed) preserved existing behavior. Is this better?

And, hey, what's the PATH (imageName) argument for in `load`? It doesn't seem to be treated as a path, nor does it actually seem to have any effect whatsoever.
That wording works for me.  Tests are still complaining...
Tests are failing all over the place; even a one-line string change (#2657). I don't think it's anything in my code.

Thanks, that did it.

Why is the magic word sometimes slash-re-test and sometimes the 'please' sentence?

@edsantiago We have three separate CI systems.

There's the legacy Papr stuff (the "Containerized (Podman in Podman)" and "Containerized (Podman in Docker)" tests), which is `bot, retest this please`

The Openshift tests (`ci/prow` tests), which are `/retest`

And the Cirrus tests, which you have to click on the "Checks" tab of the PR and then manually click the re-run button

Keeping track of this is not always easy
